The song was actually just a throwaway, but after Uzi lost his phone (which was stolen by a fan afterwards), he would release this song, alongside the other 3 songs from "Luv Is Rage 1.5". This, to prevent them from leaking. Originally titled "All My Friends Are Dead", and was recorded for the first time in January of 2017. Uzi then would re-record the song for the release version.

Notes:
(2/26/17) (Luv Is Rage 1.5 releases)
(7/24/17) (2 Luv Is 2 Rage releases)
Type: Fan-Made project using leaked files with some Luv Is Rage 2 tracks. Five of the songs were first premiered on the Shade 45 radio show of Uzi’s Generation Now label bosses DJ Drama and DJ Don Cannon in July 2017, billed as tracks that didn’t make the cut for Luv is Rage 2. The other two songs, “Repercussions” and “Like This (Alone Time),” leaked online two days apart from each other that same month. Despite this, Let You Know (Pretty Mami) and Loaded would both release.

(feat. Lil Uzi Vert) (prod. CBMIX)
Notes: Was originally released as a single by Juice. It was then reissued as track #5 on his album, "Goodbye & Good Riddance", as a late addition. The song was originally sent to Uzi with an open verse, for him to add his contribution. Which was made the same day he recorded his verse, for the "Lucid Dreams" remix. This would be the song, that would let Juice meet Uzi (who was one of his biggest inspirations, and favorite artists). On his Genius Verified for this song, Juice stated that Uzi was the perfect choice for this song; with his aggresive and crazy verse. Was exported on May 17, 2018.

OG Filename: Proud of You (Lil Uzi Vert, Young Thug, Yung Kayo)_M3_eq 04-14
Track #7 on Thug, Gunna and Young Stoner Life's collaborative album "Slime Language 2". Was originally a solo Uzi throwaway, from back when he was working on the first iteration of Eternal Atake in 2018. The track might've actually been considered for Uzi's solo project (Forever Young) at first, considering that he previewed it on his famous December, 2020 Instagram Live. But ultimately, the track was given to Thug and YSL Records for their album. The track was reissued as track #15, on the Deluxe for "Slime Language 2".

Notes: Track #5 on RED & WHITE; initially track 1 on streaming. Originally a throwaway from the first iteration of Eternal Atake, it became a huge grail in Uzi's discography, and rightfully became R&W's most popular song. A music video made in 2018, but strangely released on February 9, 2024.

(prod. Maaly Raw)

Despite the planned rollout of 'hacked' songs, this specific release caused problems between Uzi and his label, which forced him into timeout and to delay Pink Tape's release. After being taken off DSPs, it was officially delisted from SoundCloud sometime in 2023.

It interpolates Chief Keef's "Hate Bein' Sober (feat. Wiz Khalifa & 50 Cent)" and "Love Sosa". It also interpolates Gotye's worldwide known "Somebody That I Used to Know". Genius also affirms that it interpolates Travis Scott & Young Thug's "Yea Yea".

(feat. Don Toliver) (prod. Bugz Ronin, 206Derek, ​Benjamin Thomas & Matt Spatola)
(Seroquel, Confessions)
Notes: Track #20 on the Pink Tape. Benjamin Thomas explained on an interview that he initially sent the beat to Don Toliver and Uzi separately; both artists made their versions on different rooms, the same day, and ended up getting combined into one single song.

Name:
✨ Days Come and Go
(prod. Don Cannon & Tommy Parker)
Notes: Track #21 on the Pink Tape. Was thought to be recorded alongside another popular unreleased track in 2022; "Take Enough", but the lyrics suggested otherwise. Both tracks talk about Uzi's recovery after he entered rehab on 2021.

Name:
✨ Rehab
(feat. Fousheé) (prod. Brandon Finessin, Don Cannon, Bugz Ronin, Breezey Muzik & qqqu1ncy)
(Dyke, Clean The Dish)
Notes: Track #22 on the Pink Tape. Another track that talks about Uzi's recovery after being 7 months on rehab. The track had different takes, that got scrapped for this release. Once again, it has uncredited vocals done by Fousheé.

Name:
The End
(feat. BABYMETAL) (prod. KOBAMETAL & KrishnaMusic) 
[Rock Style]
Notes: Track #23 on the Pink Tape. A very experimental Japanese inspired closure for the album.

Name:
⭐ Zoom (Bonus Track)
(prod. Wheezy & Charlie Handsome)
Notes: Track #24 on the Pink Tape, listed as a bonus track. One of Uzi's biggest grails from his discography, that became a grail ever since Uzi shared a snippet of the track on the studio, back in 2017. That was when Uzi made a post after the release of Luv Is Rage 2, sharing a snippet of the song, while announcing that he was working on an early version of LUV vs. The World 2: "Just a Vibe💨 ….👨🏾‍🎤vs🌎2® ?🙄". Was also intended to be on an early draft of Eternal Atake. Has Wheezy's modern tag done by Future, and a somewhat rougher mix.

Name:
✨ Of Course (Bonus Track)
(prod. Oogie Mane, Forza & Kado)
(Of Course (Intro), Drippin' & Swaggin', Best Friend)
Notes: Track #25 on the Pink Tape, listed as a bonus track. Another big grail from Uzi's discography, that he previewed multiple times throughout the years; even on concerts. Was made when he was working on the early version of LUV vs. The World, and it was intended to be the intro for some early drafts of Eternal Atake.

Name:
Shardai (Bonus Track)
(prod. Bugz Ronin, John Ross, ISOBeats & Ike Beatz)
Notes: Track #26 on the Pink Tape, listed as a bonus track. The encoded data suggests, that this track was a very late addition to the album. MCVertt made a remix for the song, that was seemingly planned to drop eventually (probably to showcase that MC was going to be Uzi's new close producer for Luv Is Rage 3/Eternal Atake 2), but ended up getting scrapped.

Name: DPONTHEBEAT Vol 5
Notes:
(May 31, 2022) (DP Beats uploads "McLaren" to SoundCloud, which is shortly after taken down)
(July 31, 2023) (DP begins posting recently leaked grails onto his SoundCloud)
(April 3, 2025) (DP posts "Overseas Money" to SoundCloud, currently being his last uploaded song there)
Type: As a result of multiple OG Uzi x DP Beats grails leaking, specifically Recoupe (which he requested about a month prior), DP would begin posting these on his Soundcloud and was attempting to get these officially uploaded onto streaming. DP has uploaded nearly every song he's produced that has leaked since then. However, most of these tracks ended up being fully removed from streaming all together.

Notes: Released as the lead single for Khaled’s fourteenth studio album 'TIL NEXT TIME'. Samples "P.Y.T. (Pretty Young Thing)" by Michael Jackson, and "Drag Rap" by The Showboys. Interpolates "Because of You (from PxBP)" and "Delirious (feat. Soulja Boy)" by Vistoso Bosses. Was advertised with Uzi & Khaled working on the studio, but his verse was the shortest of the three rappers. Many consider that very low effort was put into this track.

Name: LP 5
Singles and throwaways

✨ Chanel Boy
(prod. ZeeGoinXrazy)
(Chanel, Double C)
Notes: What seems to be the first track for the new rollout. Was announced unexpectedly by Uzi on his Instagram: "💋ྀིྀིＣＨＡＮＥＬ ☠︎︎ ★. 2marroe !!!!! Ok bye 🛸". Was uploaded to YouTube first, and then to DSPs a couple hours later. Interpolates Metro Boomin's "Chanel Vintage (with Young Thug & Future)". The music video was recorded with the Ray-Ban Meta glasses, and the song itself features a nostalgic cadence, reminiscing of Uzi's 2017-2018 flow. Surprisingly, it was recorded on the same studio where many great 2018 tracks were made: MilkBoy studios. The track was published under the new 'Cor(e)'; which seems to be Uzi's Roc Nation imprint (could be his new label, but that remains unclarified).

Name:
⭐ Relevant
(prod. 1600Trill, Trilla & Rima Thugger)
(She Said I'm The Captain)
Notes: The second single for the new rollout. It was first posted by Uzi on his TikTok account, under the caption of "RELEVANT ´ཀ` 💋💕". It would release shortly after on YouTube, alongside its music video. This track came in with the fact that Uzi is now the third artist signed to Roc Nation's distribution platform. Alongisde Rihanna and of course, JAY-Z.

Name:
⭐ Regular
(prod. ZeeGoinXrazy)
Notes:
OG Filename: REGULAR MAIN JESS MASTER 1 28BIT 441KHZ
Third single for Uzi's new rollout. Also released under Uzi's Roc Nation imprint: Cor(e). Features a similar rythm to the ones he used during the Pink Tape era, as well as Jess Jackson's improved vocal mix. Was spontaneously posted by Uzi right before it dropped, under the caption: "💕 R E G U L A R ☠️⭐️ @ 12!!!!! Ok Bye 🛸✌🏾"; with it releasing alongside its music video. Which features clips from what is theorized to be a scrapped music video for "JUMPIN" with Carti. Interpolates Molly Santana's "Not Regular", and even shouts her out.

Name:
✨ What You Saying
(prod. MCVERTT & Larry Gold)
[Jersey Club]
Notes: Fourth single for Uzi's current rollout. It was revealed in the interview that Uzi had with Vogue that this is the actual first leading single for the upcoming album. Features a Jersey-Type beat with a somewhat Christmas-y themed production. Samples the french song by Indila, "Love Story". Following the fact that this is a lead single, Uzi actually made some hype about it with a 'formal event invitation'. The song dropped with its respective music video in collaboration with Vogue.
